zh.lifeSummary: 當Hamon de Wolverton Fitz Meinflin於1130年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾沃爾維頓時，他的父親Meinflin de Wolverton30歲，母親Mrs. Meinflin de Wolverton26歲。他於1160年與Matilda Mauduit在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1189年在他的家鄉享年59歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hamon de Wolverton Fitz Meinflin于1130年出生在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾沃爾維頓时，他的父亲Meinflin de Wolverton30岁，母亲Mrs. Meinflin de Wolverton26岁。他于1160年与Matilda Mauduit在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1189年在他的家乡享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Wolverton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Wolferton in Norfolk. The placename derives from the Old English personal name Wulfhere + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Reaney has suggested that the surname could also derive from any of the places called Wollerton, Wolverton, and Woolverton (see 2 below), yet there is very little evidence of this surname outside of East Anglia from the 15th century onward. It therefore appears that the Norfolk place is the most likely origin of present-day surname.

perhaps sometimes a habitational name from Wollerton (Shropshire), Wolverton (Buckinghamshire, Hampshire, Warwickshire, Worcestershire), and Woolverton (Somerset). The Shropshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Wulfrūn + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The Buckinghamshire, Hampshire, Worcestershire, and Somerset placenames derive from the Old English personal name Wulfhere + Old English connective -ing- + tūn. The Warwickshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Wulfweard + Old English connective -ing- + tūn.
English:

variants.name: Woolverton
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Wolverton  .

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: (i) relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + roy ‘king’, ‘son of the king’. It was often taken to imply that the original bearer was a bastard son of the king ( compare Fillary ). (ii) relationship name ‘son of Ruadh’, a Gaelic nickname meaning ‘red’, is also possible.

nameMeanings.name: Hamon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pronounced ‘ay-mon’ Anglicized spelling of Irish Éamon(n) or Éaman(n), Irish forms of Edmund . Although it still has an essentially Irish flavour, the name was popularized throughout Britain in the 1950s due to the fame of the broadcaster Eamon Andrews ( 1922–87 ).

variants.name: Éamann
meanings: Pronounced ‘ay-mon’ Anglicized spelling of Irish Éamon(n) or Éaman(n), Irish forms of Edmund . Although it still has an essentially Irish flavour, the name was popularized throughout Britain in the 1950s due to the fame of the broadcaster Eamon Andrews ( 1922–87 ).

variants.name: Eamonn
meanings: Pronounced ‘ay-mon’ Anglicized spelling of Irish Éamon(n) or Éaman(n), Irish forms of Edmund . Although it still has an essentially Irish flavour, the name was popularized throughout Britain in the 1950s due to the fame of the broadcaster Eamon Andrews ( 1922–87 ).

variants.name: Eamon
meanings: Pronounced ‘ay-mon’ Anglicized spelling of Irish Éamon(n) or Éaman(n), Irish forms of Edmund . Although it still has an essentially Irish flavour, the name was popularized throughout Britain in the 1950s due to the fame of the broadcaster Eamon Andrews ( 1922–87 ).

variants.name: Éaman
meanings: Pronounced ‘ay-mon’ Anglicized spelling of Irish Éamon(n) or Éaman(n), Irish forms of Edmund . Although it still has an essentially Irish flavour, the name was popularized throughout Britain in the 1950s due to the fame of the broadcaster Eamon Andrews ( 1922–87 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Hamon de Wolverton Fitz Meinflin was born in 1130, in Wolverton, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Meinflin de Wolverton, was 30 and his mother, Mrs. Meinflin de Wolverton, was 26. He married Matilda Mauduit in 1160, in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1189, in his hometown, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hamon de Wolverton Fitz Meinflin est né en 1130, à Wolverton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Meinflin de Wolverton, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Meinflin de Wolverton, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Matilda Mauduit en 1160, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1189, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hamon de Wolverton Fitz Meinflin 1130 in Wolverton, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Meinflin de Wolverton 30 und seine Mutter Mrs. Meinflin de Wolverton 26. Er hat 1160 in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Matilda Mauduit geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1189 im Alter von 59 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hamon de Wolverton Fitz Meinflin è nato il nel 1130 nel Wolverton, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Meinflin de Wolverton, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Meinflin de Wolverton, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Matilda Mauduit nel 1160, nel Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1189, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 59 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hamon de Wolverton Fitz Meinflinが1130年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアウオルバートンで生まれた時，父Meinflin de Wolvertonは30歳，母Mrs. Meinflin de Wolvertonは26歳であった。1160年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアでMatilda Mauduitと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1189年に故郷で59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hamon de Wolverton Fitz Meinflin(이)가 1130년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 울버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Meinflin de Wolverton(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Meinflin de Wolverton(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1160년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르에서 Matilda Mauduit(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1189년 그의 고향에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hamon de Wolverton Fitz Meinflin nasceu em 1130, em Wolverton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Meinflin de Wolverton, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Meinflin de Wolverton, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Matilda Mauduit em 1160, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1189, em sua cidade natal, com 59 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hamon de Wolverton Fitz Meinflin родился в 1130 г. в местности волвиртон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Meinflin de Wolverton было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Meinflin de Wolverton, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda Mauduit в 1160 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1189 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 59 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hamon de Wolverton Fitz Meinflin nació en 1130, en Wolverton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Meinflin de Wolverton, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Mrs. Meinflin de Wolverton, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Matilda Mauduit en 1160, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1189, en su pueblo, a la edad de 59 años.
